---
title-block-banner: false
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="homepage">

<!-- TODO: Replace with final portrait. -->
<section class="home-section hero-section" aria-labelledby="home-title">
  <div class="hero-grid">
    <div class="image-placeholder portrait-placeholder" role="img" aria-label="Portrait or Yale research photo placeholder">
      <span>Portrait / Yale research photo placeholder</span>
    </div>
    <div class="hero-copy">
      <p class="eyebrow">Computational Genomics at the Nuclear–Mitochondrial Interface</p>
      <h1 id="home-title">Li Tang</h1>
      <p class="position-placeholder">Postdoctoral Associate, Yale School of Medicine</p>
      <p class="research-identity">Nuclear–Mitochondrial Regulation <span aria-hidden="true">·</span> Mitochondrial Genetics <span aria-hidden="true">·</span> Interpretable Computational Genomics</p>
      <p class="hero-introduction">I develop interpretable computational and deep learning models to understand how nuclear genome regulation and mitochondrial genetic variation shape cellular function, clinically relevant phenotypes, and human disease.</p>
      <div class="button-row" aria-label="Primary site links">
        <a class="custom-button" href="research.html">Research</a>
        <a class="custom-button secondary-button" href="cv.html">CV</a>
        <a class="custom-button secondary-button" href="contact.html">Contact</a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="intro-section compact-intro" aria-labelledby="about-work-title">
    <div class="narrow-copy">
      <p class="eyebrow">My Research Journey</p>
      <h2 id="about-work-title">From computational genomics to mitochondrial genetics</h2>
      <p>My research journey began with the nuclear genome. Training in genomic sequence analysis, three-dimensional genome architecture, gene regulation, and deep learning gave me a computational foundation for asking mechanistic questions from complex genomic data.</p>

      <p>I now apply this foundation to mitochondrial genetics. Mitochondria have their own DNA, yet their function depends heavily on nuclear-encoded genes. I study human genetic variation across these genomes and its relationship to clinically relevant phenotypes, with attention to the population and sequencing context needed for careful variant interpretation.</p>

      <p>My future independent research program will focus on nuclear–mitochondrial regulation: integrating multimodal nuclear genomic data with mitochondrial genetic and functional features to identify the mechanisms linking the two genomes. I use AI as a tool for interpretable biological discovery—not prediction alone—so that models can generate testable explanations of regulation and disease-relevant variation.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="home-section themes-section" aria-labelledby="themes-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <p class="eyebrow">A unified research program</p>
    <h2 id="themes-title">Research Themes</h2>
  </div>
  <div class="research-directions">
    <article class="research-panel nuclear-panel">
      <!-- TODO: Replace with final 3D genome illustration. -->
      <div class="image-placeholder research-visual-placeholder genome-visual" role="img" aria-label="3D genome and chromatin architecture illustration placeholder">
        <span>3D genome / chromatin architecture illustration placeholder</span>
        <i class="genome-orbit orbit-one" aria-hidden="true"></i><i class="genome-orbit orbit-two" aria-hidden="true"></i><i class="genome-node node-one" aria-hidden="true"></i><i class="genome-node node-two" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
      <div class="research-copy">
        <p class="theme-number">01</p>
        <h3>Nuclear Genome Regulation</h3>
        <p class="theme-subtitle">Genomic sequence analysis · 3D genome architecture · gene regulation · deep learning</p>
        <p><strong>How do genome sequence and chromatin architecture organize gene regulation?</strong></p>
        <p>I build computational models that integrate sequence and genomic features to examine regulatory interactions and gene-expression programs. Interpretability is central to this work: the goal is to identify the regulatory mechanisms represented by a model and connect them to meaningful biological questions.</p>
        <a class="text-link" href="research.html#nuclear-genome-regulation-and-3d-genome-architecture">Explore this research <span aria-hidden="true">→</span></a>
      </div>
    </article>
    <div class="research-connection" aria-label="The two research directions are connected through AI and computational genomics">
      <span>Nuclear genome regulation</span><span class="connection-arrow" aria-hidden="true">↔</span><strong>Interpretable Computational Genomics</strong><span class="connection-arrow" aria-hidden="true">↔</span><span>Mitochondrial genetics</span>
    </div>
    <article class="research-panel mitochondrial-panel">
      <div class="research-copy">
        <p class="theme-number">02</p>
        <h3>Mitochondrial Genetics</h3>
        <p class="theme-subtitle">mtDNA variation · heteroplasmy · population genomics · clinically relevant phenotypes</p>
        <p><strong>How does mitochondrial genetic variation contribute to human phenotypes and disease?</strong></p>
        <p>I apply population-scale and computational genomics to study mtDNA variation, heteroplasmy, and sequencing artifacts in human data. This work aims to improve variant interpretation and clarify how mitochondrial genetic variation relates to clinically relevant phenotypes, while providing a foundation for studying nuclear–mitochondrial regulation.</p>
        <a class="text-link" href="research.html#mitochondrial-genetics-and-biology">Explore this research <span aria-hidden="true">→</span></a>
      </div>
      <!-- TODO: Replace with final mitochondrial illustration. -->
      <div class="image-placeholder research-visual-placeholder mitochondria-visual" role="img" aria-label="Mitochondrion and mitochondrial DNA illustration placeholder">
        <span>Mitochondrion / mtDNA illustration placeholder</span>
        <i class="mito-body" aria-hidden="true"></i><i class="mito-fold fold-one" aria-hidden="true"></i><i class="mito-fold fold-two" aria-hidden="true"></i><i class="mtdna-ring" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      </div>
    </article>
  </div>
</section>

</div>
```
